producer_note: Family Champagne house in Pierry (Premier Cru), making wine since 1805 and estate-bottling since 1923. Sixth generation owns 40ha across 13 communes on the southern slopes of Epernay/Sezannais.
Family house founded 1979 by Jean-Michel Gremillet in the Cote des Bar (Aube), between Balnot-sur-Laignes and Les Riceys. Owns 55ha (80% Pinot Noir); HVE 3 certified since 2020.
Chambolle-Musigny domaine run by siblings Gilbert and Christine Felettig since the 1990s, expanded from father Henri's 2ha (1965) to over 12ha across 130 parcels in the Cote de Nuits.
New Zealand's oldest continuously operating winery (since 1895), based in Havelock North. Sources fruit across Havelock Hills, Bridge Pa Triangle and Dartmoor Valley sub-regions of Hawke's Bay.
Iconic 4th-generation family estate at the foot of Simonsberg, Stellenbosch. Began bottling under the Kanonkop label in 1973; renowned for Pinotage and the Paul Sauer Bordeaux blend.
Pioneer Vinsobres family estate since 1905, run by Pascal and Richard Jaume across 100ha around the village facing Mont Ventoux. Produces Cotes du Rhone and Vinsobres cru wines; HVE certified.
Frederic Savart's grower estate based in Ecueil (1er Cru) on the Montagne de Reims. Took over family vines in 2005, farms ~4ha of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay across Premier Cru parcels.
Family grower founded 1978 by Jean-Marie Goulard in Prouilly on the Massif de Saint-Thierry (NW Reims). 4th-generation Damien Goulard farms ~7-8ha, converting to organic.
Cool-climate Grampians estate established 1969 by the Fratin brothers; vines in granitic soils against the Great Dividing Range. Famed for the Langton's-classified Langi Shiraz.
Grower estate founded 1986 in Flavigny, near Avize. Adrien Dhondt farms ~6ha of Chardonnay in Cramant (Grand Cru), Cuis (1er Cru), Chouilly and Sezannais using organic/biodynamic methods.
Moretti Cuseri family estate since the 1950s; 60ha of vines in Tuscany producing Oreno and Crognolo IGT plus Chianti Superiore. Organic; vineyards span multiple Tuscan zones.

Founded 1966 by German-born Wolfgang Blass in Nuriootpa, Barossa Valley. Major brand (Treasury Wine Estates) sourcing fruit across Barossa, Eden Valley, Langhorne Creek and other South Australian regions.
One-man grower estate working a single 2.5ha vineyard (Le Mont Benoit) in Villers-aux-Noeuds, just outside Reims. Took over in 1997; certified organic since 2011, ~7,500 bottles.
Manetti family estate purchased 1968 in Panzano's Conca d'Oro. Certified organic; 100+ha of Chianti Classico vines, famed for Flaccianello della Pieve Sangiovese.
Faro family estate founded 2005 on the northern slopes of Mount Etna in Solicchiata. 30ha at 650-950m altitude across Contrade Rampante, Zottorinoto, Santo Spirito and Feudo di Mezzo.

Parker Coonawarra Estate founded 1985 by John Parker on Coonawarra's terra rossa strip near Penola. ~17ha of Cabernet Sauvignon-led plantings; Langton's Classified V producer of Bordeaux-variety reds.

Armenier-family domaine founded 1989 (vines held for generations); biodynamic pioneer farming 18ha in Chateauneuf-du-Pape, plus parcels in Lirac and Cotes-du-Rhone. Flagship CDP Vieilles Vignes from old Grenache.
Founded 1993, headquartered in Chimbarongo, Colchagua. ~1,246ha across 10 estates in Limari, Aconcagua, Casablanca, San Antonio, Cachapoal, Maipo, Maule and Bio-Bio; Pinot Noir specialist.

Coonawarra's oldest winery, with heritage tracing to John Riddoch's 1896 vintage in the historic woolshed near Penola. Modern Katnook Estate launched 1980; ~155ha of Cabernet, Shiraz, Merlot and whites on terra rossa.

Family estate founded 1960 by Giorgio Lungarotti in Torgiano (DOCG); now run by daughters Chiara and Teresa Severini. 230ha at Torgiano (VIVA-certified) plus 20ha at Montefalco (organic); ~2.5M bottles annually.

Historic Florentine family making wine since 1308; nine estates across Tuscany including Castello Nipozzano (Chianti Rufina), Tenuta Perano (Chianti Classico), CastelGiocondo (Brunello), Pomino and Maremma's Tenuta Ammiraglia.

Casarena Bodega y Vinedos in Lujan de Cuyo, housed in a restored 1937 winery. Owns four single-vineyard estates (Naoki, Lauren, Owen, Jamilla) across Agrelo and Perdriel; certified vegan and sustainable.

Napa Valley producer with tasting room at 3105 Silverado Trail (opened 2021), led by veteran winemaker Linda Trotta. Focus on accessible Cabernet, Chardonnay, Pinot Noir and Merlot from California sources.
Founded 1902 by Giulio Ferrari in Trento; benchmark Trentodoc traditional-method sparkling-wine producer. 100% Chardonnay-based cuvees from high-altitude Alpine slopes; Lunelli family-owned.

Grower estate founded 1965 by Henri Seleque in Pierry; 3rd-generation Jean-Marc took over in 2008. ~9ha across 45 parcels in seven villages including Pierry, Moussy, Epernay, Mardeuil, Dizy, Boursault and Vertus.
Sparkling-wine brand owned by Sovereign Brands (NY). Belaire Rose is produced in Provence from Syrah/Grenache/Cinsault, while other cuvees are made in Burgundy; not a single-estate winery.

Wagner family Napa Valley winery in Rutherford, founded 1972 with land farmed since the 1940s. Renowned for Cabernet Sauvignon, particularly the Special Selection (1984 was Wine Spectator Wine of the Year).

brand: Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande
producer_note: Pauillac 2eme Cru Classe (1855), with origins in the 17th-century Pichon estate split in 1850. Owned since 2006 by the Rouzaud family (Louis Roederer); plantings 61% Cabernet Sauvignon, 32% Merlot.

House founded 1901 by Ernest Rapeneau in Hautvillers as a wine merchant, specialising in Champagne from 1927. Part of the Rapeneau family group sourcing from ~420 acres of estate vineyards across Champagne.

13-generation Alsace house founded 1626, based in Ribeauville since post-WWI. Owns 60ha mainly in Ribeauville, Bergheim and Hunawihr (incl. 1.67ha of Rosacker Grand Cru for Clos Sainte Hune). Famed for dry Riesling.

Estate launched 1995 as a Frescobaldi-Mondavi joint venture (now wholly Frescobaldi-owned). 30ha of vines in Montalcino; pioneered Sangiovese-Merlot blending under the Toscana IGT 'Luce' label, plus Brunello.
Family grower estate founded 1947 by Roger Pouillon in Mareuil-sur-Ay; now run by Fabrice Pouillon. 6.5ha across 36 parcels in Ay Grand Cru, Mareuil-sur-Ay and Epernay; Pinot-Noir-driven.
Brunier family estate since 1891 (six generations) on the Plateau de la Crau in southeast Chateauneuf-du-Pape. Iconic La Crau cuvee from pebble-strewn terroir; classic GSM blend aged in foudres.

Fattoria di Valiano, a 230ha (75ha vines) organic estate in Castelnuovo Berardenga, Chianti Classico DOCG. Part of the Piccini 1882 group; Sangiovese-led portfolio including Chianti Classico Gran Selezione 'San Lazzaro'.
Pioneering Chianti Classico estate founded 1974 above Greve in Chianti. Organic since 1988 and biodynamic since 2000; ~50ha of vegan-certified vines in Greve, Radda and Lamole, plus 40ha in Maremma.
